overview.description: The Teucrium Sugar Fund (CANE) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the Teucrium Sugar Fund Benchmark index. The fund tracks an index of sugar futures contracts. It reflects the performance of sugar by holding ICE Futures US sugar futures contracts with three different expiration dates. CANE was launched on Sep 19, 2011 and is issued by Teucrium.

data.title: Cocoa Prices Jump Over 15% as Investors Reposition
data.text: EXCLUSIVE: Cocoa is on course to rise by 15% after hitting its highest level in over three months this week, though prices are unlikely to return to levels seen in 2025, analysts say.

data.title: Widespread Drought Adds to Stresses for U.S. Farmers. Food Prices Are a Focus.
data.text: Just in time for the start of the growing season, the National Drought Mitigation Center's latest data says that 51% of U.S. states and Puerto Rico are in moderate drought or worse.

data.title: Grain Futures Drop After USDA Increases Crop-Supply Estimates
data.text: The latest monthly World Agricultural Supply and Demand Estimates report shows higher ending stocks for corn and soybeans, running counter to what markets expected.

data.title: A Sweet ETF Ready for Valentine's Day
data.text: Scarlet Fu looks at an ETF focusing on sugar as chocolate gets more expensive for this upcoming Valentine's Day.

data.title: Why sugar prices have risen nearly 40% so far this year
data.text: Sugar prices have climbed by nearly 40% so far this year, on track to mark their best yearly performance in 14 years, with U.S. and global supplies expected to remain tight.
